LIV has always been the best software when it comes to content creation and somehow it got even better!?

reviews.review_title: A very promising VR streaming app!
reviews.review_description:
I tried LIV HUB, and I have to say it’s a very convenient and user-friendly VR streaming tool. The setup process is simple, and you can start streaming in just a few steps. However, I have a few suggestions that I believe could make it even better:

1. Viewer interaction is essential
A built-in multi-platform chat window (YouTube, Twitch, etc.) that can appear on the wrist or as a movable floating panel would greatly enhance interaction during streams.

2. Better notification system
It would be amazing if gift alerts, new followers, and subscription notifications could pop up on the wrist UI or floating screen for easier real-time response.

3. More control over stream details
Currently, the interface only has a basic start/pause button. Adding the ability to edit stream titles, descriptions, and categories directly inside VR would make the workflow smoother.

4. TikTok streaming support
Since TikTok is a major global platform, adding TT support would help creators reach a wider audience.

5. Multi-platform streaming
Allowing users to stream to multiple platforms simultaneously would be a huge advantage for content creators.

6. TTS chat integration
A text-to-speech chat option would help streamers stay engaged with their audience without missing messages while in VR.

Overall, LIV HUB is already a great and promising VR streaming tool. With a few more features, it has the potential to become the top choice for VR content creators.

reviews.review_title: It logged me off of my vr and turned off
reviews.review_description: This app sucks never use it I was playing gorilla tag and my game froze and logged me off of my vr

Before I use this I want to make sure of 2 things, does this really work on every single game or just the ones that have the LIV tablet already in the game (e.g. gorilla tag, worst food truck, skelly, etc.), and if it works with every game, can it capture game sounds, because for some reason fnaf help wanted 1 and 2 don’t capture sound when I record them, so I wanna know if this fixes that problem? (I don’t really mind if it does not fix the problem, that’s fine, I’ll just find another way. But I am curious if it works on every game)

developer_response.body:
LIV Hub provides 2 methods of streaming.

LIV Camera streaming only works with games that have added the LIV Camera and streaming, that list can be found on our discord.  LIV.tv/discord.  LIV Camera games get all mic and game audio.

Headset streaming works with any app on the Meta Quest. However, it's limited with mic audio capture due to the Quest OS restricting only 1 source using mic audio, that's either the game or LIV Hub for streaming.

reviews.review_description: Really good

When I was live streaming on YouTube, I thought I was going live. I thought I did everything correct but I didn’t. My channel never had the live stream uploaded so if anybody that works from this place￼ liv just please tell me how to do this how to fix this and please fix this￼

developer_response.body:
Hi, our Alpha has some quirks to get started with YouTube.  You’ll need to follow the below process to go live but a soon to be released update will solve these steps.
1. Open YouTube Studio in your browser.
2. Click Create in the top-right corner.
   - Select Go live.
3. Allow the streaming dashboard to fully load.
4. Verify your stream title and visibility settings.
5. Start your stream from LIV Hub or from the in-game LIV camera.
